tokens: (Probably looking at his checklist) Okay; pan one, up on Spook crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: My hammer just got hung up in the instrument panel.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It was on those stanchions there.
I'm sorry, I should have said something to you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; well, I won't need it (meaning the hammer).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Do you) want to put this thing (meaning his purge-valve pin) back in one more time.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You start the LPM (Lunar Portable Magnetometer), and I'll be over in just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'll get the (high-gain) antenna up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Are you on a ray there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The blocks are angular, but they are definitely coming out of Buster.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Lost in the static, which then clears) dissipate very quickly.
In fact, they don't even come to Flag (probably means Spook).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, we've got a TV picture!
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Buster is a real impressive crater.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Right...Just to the left...Right under the Sun, as a matter of fact.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: By golly, we did park it in the right place!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Passing on an urgent message from Experiments via the Flight Director) Okay, John.
We'll need to press on with that
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John) How's your pointing from that...?
(Pause) Okay; since you don't...(Since you) just do LPM, John and I'm gonna do the 500, I won't put your bag back on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Under here, again, right under the regolith, the first centimeter or so, we have the white albedo material.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To John) Hey, that does a good job on the lens there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Your eye looks clear to me here, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the Earth is...You should be locked up, I guess.
And the Earth is boresighted in the boresight machine.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) That's gonna be cross-Sun, so I'm going to do it at f/8.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That (meaning Spook)'s a deep monster, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Doesn't sound like they're reading us.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) (If you) read, wag your TV.
(Will you) fix this thing for me?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I've got to do this LPM.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You don't want to go ahead and get started with that?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (A little sharply) I want us to do it, but I...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John and Charlie, this is Houston.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, you've been there all the time.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What am I hung up on now, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, you got on that what-cha-ma-call-it, that...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to take a few (pictures) of South Ray here, with the 500.
We got a good view of it here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the LPM has got its sensor head on it, and the power switch is coming on Mark.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Tony, the 500 millimeter's up to 50.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I guess I take a few too many.
I had a triple, vertical, stereo pan on Stone Mountain, and about five frames on old South Ray.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm finished with my pan, and the 500.
I'm gonna run over to Buster and do some sampling.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Buster is really an impressive crater, Houston.
The walls are so darn steep, and the blocks are over it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Charlie, sounds like you've got a good plan there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) And if you'll notice, I'm carrying John's bag, and the shovel, and I'm not taking the gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, that's gonna be a little steep ridge to climb (up to the rim of Buster).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Man, John, I tell you, this is some sight up here, looking down into that beauty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Slightly winded from the climb) Tony, the blocks in Buster are covered...(Correcting himself) The bottom is covered with blocks - the largest 5 meters across.
The blocks seem to be (in) a preferred orientation, northeast to southwest.
They go all the way up the wall on those two sides; and on the other sides, you can only barely see them outcropping in about 5 percent.
Ninety percent of the bottom is covered with blocks that are 50 centimeters and larger.
And I'll get a partial pan into there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It makes it sound like a secondary.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Houston, I'm back (at the Rover).
The sensor number 1 is on there and...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, if that is a secondary, that is a rock that hit in there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The rocks down there are very fractured, though.
The rocks down there are extremely fractured.
You can see a major fracture set running...(correcting himself) dipping about north 30 degrees on one rock.
The other one is subhorizontal, so it's just a very impressive sight as far as the boulder goes.
Some of them...Well, I call some of them subrounded, but the majority of them are angular.
And they have a grayish texture to them and that's about all I can tell.
I got a partial pan from up here on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And why don't you press on sampling now?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, it's time (to get an LPM reading), John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Responding to Tony's transmission at 124:33:21) All right.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; (are) you going to give me...(Hearing Tony's go-ahead) Okay, X, 657; Y, 363; Z, 440.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did you get all that stuff, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, there's a sort of angular to subrounded block going in bag number 5, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's dust covered so that's all I can say about it.
I got another little one, same spot; and it's got a whitish clast (means 'cast') to it, and another little one that's got a whitish cast to the underside of it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And John, verify your visor's down.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I never...I forgot to raise it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Tony, I really think we're sampling blocks out of this...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And did you get the picture of the (LPM) location, there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: From here is a soil sample going into bag 6.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is on the rim of Buster.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And John, you can take a reading whenever you want.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That was frame count 56 on magazine Baker...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's the rock I want, but it's too big for the bag (meaning it is too big for an individual sample bag).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: But it might go in the bag.
How much time (until we have to leave), Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay: 114 is X, 361 is Y, 356 is Z.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: 116 is X, 360 is Y, 366 is Z.
(Pause) 116 is X, 357 is Y, 374 is Z.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Going out and turning it around.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did you get all of that (meaning the readings)?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Charlie, we'll be leaving as soon as John's finished.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, there's another rock going into bag 7.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I'm going about a quarter of a diameter away from Buster and sample some more.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The rim of Buster is a pretty good slope, climbing up there, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: How long have we been out, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I got a couple hours, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I've got (lost under Tony) six hours.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And I've got a Mark, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: John, the only trouble is that you can't put the bag...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, John, (you can read the LPM).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (In the midst of the juggling act) Agh!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Outstanding and visor down (now that the LPM readings are done).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony, the rock I've got here...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (LPM) Read switch is Off, and the Power switch is Off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (It) is a very friable rock, and it's the most shocked rock I've ever seen; it's just pure white.
The whole matrix is pure white.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I hate to tell you this, but I dropped my bags.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If you come...They came...Guess what happened?
The little thing; I didn't...It came unlocked.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It was just the top thing.
(Pause) This is really some rock, really shocked.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, move back and let me get it on the fly, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, and as soon as you get buttoned up there, we'll be taking off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To John, under Tony, commenting on John's kneeling prowess) Just beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, on this friable rock, this shocked one.
It's very friable and I'm gonna try and get it in the bag but I'm not sure it's gonna go.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And if I don't get it in the bag, I don't think it's going to survive.
Well, there's part of it in the bag, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It just broke in two in my hand.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Geochemists are always telling us how little rock they need.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: They're going to get a lot of nice rock samples today.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Sounds like a lot of rocks.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That was a real balancing act, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And if somebody cleans our suits, they can get another 5 pounds.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now, here comes the interesting part.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ed (Mitchell) was right, it does wind up on itself.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's like a bowl of spaghetti!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What I hope is, it (meaning the loops) doesn't end up going into the mouth of the (reel)...(Pause) (Chuckles) (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, you're doing pretty good there, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; bag number 10, Tony, is another one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, we better get you loaded back up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I was worried about that power cab(le)...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I was worried about...On the geophone experiment...(Hearing Tony) Huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: They want us to load up, John.
I guess they're...I'm running out of water.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, go ahead with what you were saying.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I said...Have we got a problem of some kind?
Are we needing to get Charlie back?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're just trying to...You're a little bit late on this station and, to get everything in and so to get back in on time, we might be a little late.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We are going to shorten the EVA by about 8 minutes, is all.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We got four (rock) samples and about...That (soil) sample is going into bag 11, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And here, Tony, I don't really see...(Picking up the SCB and feeling the weight) Man, that's a sackful, John!
(Pause) (To Tony) I don't see the high albedo stuff underneath.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, that thing (meaning the LPM) is in as good as I can put it back in, Houston.
But I wouldn't be surprised to see it hanging out some day.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Houston, I hate to tell you this, but those rocks, these light ones here, (chuckling) they look like caliche to me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) And could I get your final frame count?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I gave you the last rate count on the...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the old pallet (the gate on the back of the Rover) is closed.
Locks right in there; look at that beauty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause as Charlie gets into his seat) There we go.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...can you check and make sure your purge valve pin's still in?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Ain't no way I could tell, unless you...Yeah, it's there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) (Playing verbally) We are mounting upon the vehicle!
transcript.speaker: Duke
